question.text: Under the SEBI (FPI) Regulations, 2019, the registration granted to a foreign portfolio investor by the designated depository participant on behalf of SEBI shall be:

answer.text: Valid for 1 year and subject to annual renewal
Valid for 5 years and renewable thereafter
Valid for 3 years and renewable thereafter
Permanent, unless suspended or cancelled by SEBI or surrendered by the FPI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the SEBI (FPI) Regulations, 2019, subject to compliance with the provisions of the Act, registration granted by the DDP on behalf of SEBI shall be permanent unless suspended or cancelled by SEBI or surrendered by the FPI. **The Trap:** Earlier FPI regulations (2014) had a validity period, but the 2019 regulations made FPI registration permanent. Candidates familiar with older regulations may select a fixed validity period. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While registration is permanent, FPIs must continue to comply with all conditions. Suspension and cancellation are dealt with under Chapter V of SEBI Intermediaries Regulation, 2008.
